authoritative_sources.title: Department of Cannabis Control Regulations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's current regulations are authoritative for product quality plans, manufacturing protocols, batch production records, track-and-trace requirements, quality assurance review, laboratory testing, remediation, and failed-batch disposition in a major regulated cannabis market.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: jurisdiction-aware cannabis quality and release-boundary review
used_for: product quality plan and batch-record expectations
quality assurance review, COA, remediation, and failed-batch boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Product Quality Plan FAQ
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's FAQ is authoritative guidance for how cannabis manufacturers should define hazards, controls, ingredient specifications, label-claim accuracy, and batch-production expectations in a product quality plan.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: hazard and control definition for cannabis quality review

authoritative_sources.title: Representative Sampling Checklist for Distributors
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's checklist is authoritative workflow guidance for representative sampling, chain-of-custody handling, post-test review, and destruction or remediation expectations after failed cannabis testing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: sampling, chain-of-custody, and test-ready package review

authoritative_sources.title: Form 24: Data Package Cover Page and Checklist
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DCC's data-package checklist is authoritative for the analytical evidence bundle, review signatures, method references, and record completeness expected when cannabis quality decisions rely on laboratory results.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: laboratory data-package and COA support review

authoritative_sources.title: Marijuana Testing Requirements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OHA's testing page is authoritative statewide guidance for accredited laboratory use, passing-test requirements, failed-test reporting, reanalysis and retest limits, and destruction or remediation status.

authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis Sampling Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OHA's sampling guide is authoritative workflow guidance for cannabis batch sizes, primary and duplicate samples, sample integrity, and product-form-specific sampling expectations.

authoritative_sources.title: Massachusetts Cannabis Control Commission Tightens Testing Requirements in Continued Effort to Enhance Industry Public Health and Safety
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The CCC announcement is authoritative for Massachusetts testing-program controls including single independent testing laboratory expectations, COA reporting discipline, and Commission visibility through Metrc.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: current testing-program control and reporting review

authoritative_sources.title: Metrc
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Colorado MED's Metrc page is authoritative for the use of the primary inventory tracking system of record and for package-level administrative hold, recall, and traceability functionality in licensed marijuana operations.
used_for: administrative hold and recall feature awareness

authoritative_sources.title: Committee D37 on Cannabis - Scope
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ASTM Committee D37 is the recognized public standards forum dedicated to cannabis laboratories, processing, and quality topics, making it directly relevant to cannabis-specific control expectations and terminology.

authoritative_sources.title: Cannabis & Hemp
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AOAC's Cannabis Analytical Science Program is a recognized cannabis-specific methods and proficiency framework for potency and contaminant testing, directly relevant to evidence quality and analytical confidence.

authoritative_sources.title: FDA Regulation of Cannabis and Cannabis-Derived Products, Including Cannabidiol (CBD)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA's cannabis regulation page is authoritative federal guidance for therapeutic-claim and drug-approval boundaries that cannabis quality work must not overstate or cross into.

workflow_stages.name: cannabis quality intake and jurisdiction triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify jurisdiction, license type, program context, product form, affected batch or package, requested action, and whether the request stays inside cannabis-quality evidence support before taking action.

workflow_stages.name: seed-to-sale truth-set and artifact assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect the track-and-trace events, product quality plan, master manufacturing protocol, batch production record, package identifiers, hold status, and label-control records needed to establish the authoritative cannabis quality record set.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: sampling, chain-of-custody, and laboratory evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate representative sampling, chain-of-custody continuity, laboratory data packages, COAs, method suitability, and analytical result quality before using test evidence to support disposition.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, remediation, and nonconformance handling
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether visible quality and safety evidence supports preserving hold, giving a bounded release recommendation, escalating remediation review, or blocking closure without implying final authority.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: complaint, incident, and authority escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate repeat defects, failed tests, complaint patterns, adverse events, legal or medical questions, multiple-COA conflicts, and recall or regulator-facing questions that move ownership outside the lane.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: product quality plans, master manufacturing protocols, and batch production records
harvest batches, process lots, package identifiers, and seed-to-sale event histories
representative-sampling records, chain-of-custody records, and sample integrity notes
certificates of analysis, laboratory data packages, method references, and result-review signoffs
quality holds, administrative holds, nonconformance reports, remediation records, and destruction logs
label specifications, packaging proofs, potency variance checks, and product catalog or package metadata
CAPA records, complaint files, adverse-event notes, and recall or quarantine notices
approved-laboratory lists, accreditation or approval records, and regulator-facing submission references

allowed: classify cannabis-quality requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites

prepare evidence-backed quality or safety summaries, hold or nonconformance records, findings summaries, and escalation packets tied to named cannabis batch, package, chain-of-custody, COA, and incident records
map seed-to-sale, sampling, laboratory, remediation, complaint, and release dependencies to the correct owner without improvising legal, medical, laboratory-director, or final release authority
escalate: required tenant facts, jurisdiction, product classification, seed-to-sale records, laboratory evidence, or source context are missing and materially change the outcome
public-source, jurisdictional, and tenant-policy conflicts materially change whether product can be held, released, remediated, retested, transferred, or closed

repeat defects, adverse events, multiple conflicting COAs, failed-test posture, complaint severity, remediation questions, or legal or medical boundaries require laboratory, compliance, legal, medical, or recall-command review
refuse: requests for final product release, laboratory signoff, remediation approval, recall command, legal interpretation, medical advice, or regulator-facing statements
requests to release or close work when required chain-of-custody, package lineage, COA support, data-package review, CAPA evidence, or approvals are incomplete or disputed
requests that would bypass documented cannabis sampling, testing, hold, track-and-trace, complaint, or remediation controls

domain_failure_modes: Treating a COA as sufficient even though the package identifier, chain of custody, data package, or method context does not support it.
Releasing cannabis product even though the track-and-trace system still shows administrative hold, failed-test, or unresolved remediation posture.
Normalizing multiple conflicting COAs or lab-shopping behavior into a confident release answer.
Using generic manufacturing QA language that ignores cannabis-specific product quality plans, batch production records, package lineage, and jurisdiction-specific testing rules.
Failing to escalate complaints, adverse events, or repeated contaminant failures that may require recall command, medical review, or regulator communication.
Blurring cannabis quality review into legal interpretation of hemp versus marijuana, interstate movement, controlled-substance status, or therapeutic claims.
Closing remediation or nonconformance work without CAPA effectiveness evidence, complaint lookback, or authorized review support.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Safety or quality evidence is incomplete or contradictory
Release is requested while a hold or unresolved issue exists
Incident handling requires licensed, safety, or formal QA authority
Evidence is incomplete for release or signoff
A user tries to close quality work without review support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Federal versus state cannabis legality and interstate movement
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack treats state regulator workflows as the operational baseline for licensed cannabis quality work and routes federal legality or interstate commerce interpretation to legal or compliance owners.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Adult-use versus medical program rules and product-classification differences
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack requires jurisdiction, program type, and product classification as tenant-supplied prerequisites and refuses to improvise when those differences would change testing or release posture.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State-specific retest, remediation, and multiple-COA rules
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack preserves the public baseline that retest, remediation, and COA handling are jurisdiction-specific and escalates rather than harmonizing conflicting state and tenant rules.

notes: The public baseline supports cannabis-specific quality evidence handling, but tenant retrieval is still required for exact jurisdiction, product scope, approved laboratory context, release owners, remediation rules, and system connectors.

The specialist is intentionally structured as cannabis-quality evidence support and must not imply final product release, laboratory signoff, legal advice, medical advice, or recall-command authority.
